DEFENDANTS BARRETT BLOCK PARTNERS AND JOHN BARRETT'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AWARDING DEFENDANTS $11,000.00 IN COSTS AND ATTORNEYS' FEES (WHICH INCLUDES ADDITIONAL TIME INCURRED). THE COURT HAS REDUCED THE REQUEST BY 20% TO REFLECT THE FACT THAT THE CONTRACT CAUSES OF ACTION REMAIN. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANTS' PLEADINGS HAD A COMMON THEME, THE PAPERS DIRECTED MATERIAL ARGUMENTS TO HAVE THE CONTRACT CLAIMS DISMISSED. GIVEN THE COURT'S RULING ON THOSE CLAIMS, A REDUCTION IS WARRANTED. PARTIES ARE TO SUBMIT FURTHER BRIEFING (NOT TO EXCEED 5 PAGES) BY 5:00 PM ON JULY 17TH ON THE ISSUE OF THE STAY(S). THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO JULY 17, 2008, AT WHICH TIME THE MATTER WILL BE DEEMED SUBMITTED. JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: KIMBERLY JENNINGS, CSR 11452

MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Barrett Block Partners and John Barrett's Motion for Attorney's Fees is deemed submitted by Judge Mahoney. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Jul-10-2008 Continued To Jul-17-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BARRETT BLOCK PARTNERS AND JOHN BARRETT'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES

Appearances:
James Kraus of Zacks Utrecht & Leadbetter (956-8100) for defendants and moving parties
Julian Lastowski (863-8874) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: The motion is granted awarding defendants $11,000.00 in costs and attorneys' fees (which includes additional time incurred). The Court has reduced the request by 20% to reflect the fact that the contract causes of action remain. Although defendants' pleadings had a common theme, the papers directed material arguments to have the contract claims dismissed. Given the Court's ruling on those claims, a reduction is warranted. Parties are to submit further briefing (not to exceed 5 pages) by 5:00 pm on July 17th on the issue of the stay(s). The matter is continued to July 17, 2008, at which time the matter will be deemed submitted. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Kimberly Jennings, CSR 11452
